description: Dotfiles changes accumulate and require manual sessions to group and commit them; a periodic auto-commit script could handle this.
Manual drift analysis sessions compare live Mint system state against NixOS config to find uncaptured customizations before they're lost.
User discovers disk space pressure reactively rather than being alerted proactively; periodic monitoring with alerts could prevent surprise.
Configuration changes (notification sounds, keybindings, etc.) are applied manually to both Mint and NixOS rather than being driven from a single declarative source.
User has to manually check whether personal automation tools (autodoro, cron jobs) are running and correctly configured, rather than having a health dashboard or alerting.
